title: CRISPR learns to read tumor DNA as Mythos fractures government - TCR 04/20/26
description:
ThermoCas9, a CRISPR enzyme published in Nature, reads tumor DNA methylation to cut only cancerous cells while Anthropic's Mythos fractures U.S. government posture as the NSA deploys the model the Pentagon banned.
This episode covers the ThermoCas9 breakthrough, a three-amino-acid supplement pushing gene editing to 90% efficiency, the Mythos governance arc across four continents, Chinese tech workers building AI counter-sabotage tools, data centers becoming a ballot-box issue, and solar leading global energy growth.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-20-2026/

0:00 Open and framing
0:36 ThermoCas9 reads tumor DNA methylation
3:59 Three-amino-acid supplement boosts CRISPR to 90% efficiency
7:11 NSA deploys Mythos despite Pentagon ban
9:58 Chinese tech workers build AI counter-sabotage tools
12:37 Data centers become a midterm election issue
14:31 What shipped
15:12 Close

A joint Wageningen University and Van Andel Institute team published findings on ThermoCas9, a CRISPR variant that reads methylation patterns on human DNA to physically distinguish tumor cells from healthy cells - cutting only the cancerous sequences without external instruction. A CZ Biohub study found that adding three common amino acids alongside lipid nanoparticles boosted mRNA delivery 20-fold and pushed CRISPR editing efficiency from 25% to nearly 90% in a single dose, solving a bottleneck the field had been attacking from the wrong direction. The NSA is actively using Anthropic's Mythos Preview for cybersecurity despite the Pentagon's own supply-chain risk designation against the company, while Australia's ASIC became the fourth continent's financial regulator to publicly confirm monitoring of the model. Anthropic's annualized revenue tripled to over $30 billion, outpacing OpenAI for the first time. MIT Technology Review documented Chinese tech workers being instructed to build AI replicas of their own roles, with a viral counter-project offering deliberate sabotage tools that gathered five million likes. NPR confirmed data center opposition has crossed into midterm electoral politics, with incumbents losing seats over approvals in multiple states. A Rice University protein-display platform published in *Nature Biotechnology* generates over 10 million activity data points per experiment, and the IEA confirmed solar as the single largest contributor to global energy supply growth in 2025.

https://sharedsapience.com/century-report/
